-- 1 of 5 --

"Crazy Like a Fox"
When I first started in business I started publishing about the stock market. That was
something that I had some expertise in. I had spent a lot of time studying the stock
market, but I didn’t have any credentials. I didn’t have any letters after my name. I
wasn’t a stock broker. I wasn’t a registered investment advisor. I didn’t have any
degrees in the stock market or anything. It was just something I had studied a lot.
!
I was desperate to start a business. I had learned about this whole idea of selling
information online, so I wanted to try it. It was something I knew about, so I started
publishing a newsletter.
!
Truth be told, I didn’t really have the wherewithal to sell something. I didn’t know how
to sell stuff. I didn’t have the self-confidence to start selling something, since I didn’t
have any letters after my name or anything like that.
!
So I just started publishing a newsletter. The first newsletter I sent out to all my friends
and family who had an email address. There were 19 email addresses for that first
newsletter, and actually one of those was my second email address and one was my
wife’s email address. Outside of my immediate family, 17 people got that newsletter.
!
I gradually built that up from there and it grew through word of mouth. I kept on
publishing this newsletter... and eventually I came out with paid versions of the
newsletter... but I always kept that free version of the newsletter.
!
At that time, I had this friend who had been in the business of publishing stock market
newsletters for a long time. In fact, his dad was in the business and that’s how he came
into the business. I remember him telling me that he couldn’t figure out why I was
publishing this free newsletter, because it was the best quality newsletter I could put
out at the time. It became pretty darn popular and actually grew through word of
mouth.
!
He just thought it was insane that I was publishing this newsletter for free. He couldn’t
figure it out. He was charging $1,000 per month for his newsletter. He was like, “Why
would you do this?” He had this long history of publishing before the internet. It went
way back, and his dad’s newsletter went way back, and he couldn't figure it out.
!
A couple years later he said, “I used to think you were crazy, but it was sort of crazy
like a fox.” He could see I had built up this big platform and I had built up this big list.
!
In fact, he came around asking to partner with me to try to get my expertise. It was
just because of the strategy I had developed, and maybe it was because my business
grew up in the online era of giving away good information that eventually led into a
sale.
!

PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 2 of 5 --

That one strategy has been a big win for me. It also taught me the real power of
strategy in your business. Sun Tzu, in the The Art of War, wrote “The battle is always
won before you set foot on the battlefield.” That’s really Product Launch Formula in one
sentence.
! “The battle is always won before you set foot on the battlefield.”
!
In other words, strategy is what wins it for you.
!
In Product Launch Formula, we give away great content and we build up trust. We build
up rapport and a relationship with the people that are getting our materials, that are on
our lists in the launch, and it sets up that sale. It’s a lot easier to sell when someone
trusts you. The launch is all about everything that leads up to the sale. That’s the battle
that’s won before you set foot on the battlefield. Before someone gets to your sales
letter, you’ve already won the battle, because they already trust you. That’s really what
Product Launch Formula is all about.
!
That’s what I did when I started publishing that newsletter. Even though I was giving
away this free newsletter, I was setting up the eventual sale. I put in the effort ahead of
time. I used that strategy of thinking further out than just trying to get the money right
now.
!
Product Launch Formula is about setting up that sale, but it’s also bigger.
!
It’s about making decisions for the long term. That’s the mindset I want you to get in as
we start Module 1. It’s not about making the money right now, it’s not about sending
that email and instantly how much money we’re going to make right now. It’s about
setting yourself up in a business that’s going to last.
!
This one thing will always set you apart, because so many other people are making the
money today. Their long term is making the money this week. If you just think a little
bit longer term and use your prelaunch to build that trust and rapport... use each
launch to build onto the next... then you are going to win.
!
Product Launch Formula is great, because the PLF tactics can give you a quick cash
infusion. I’ve done this over and over. I’ve used it to finish my basement. I’ve used it as
a down payment on a new house. I’ve used it to pay my tax bill. Long term it’s the
strategy of sharing great content and creating trust before you ask for the sale... the
strategy of getting great results for your clients and customers... creating great
products based on that feedback that you’re getting from the market through your
launch conversation. It’s about putting together great promotions that cause great
leverage for you that bring in a lot of money. But also it could bring in a lot of money
for your partners.
!

-- 3 of 5 --

Those strategies all create huge leverage going forward and they continually win. That
leverage builds on top of each other. It’s this big snowball rolling downhill, where your
launches get bigger and bigger.
!
You need to think about, “How will this action set up my next action?” In other words,
“What’s the purpose of this email?”
!
It may seem like it’s free to send that email to your list, but there’s always some cost
involved. Some people are going to unsubscribe... or if they look at your email and it’s
not impactful or meaningful to them, then maybe they won’t open your next email. So
there’s always some cost involved. What are you going to get out of that email? What’s
the purpose of that email, that blog post, that video? What’s the purpose of that
product? What is it going to get you now and in the future?
!
I always like to think about things on multiple time frames. “I’m sending this email,
what’s it going to do for me now? A week from now? A month from now? A year from
now?” I’m always thinking that. When I’m in a launch I’m thinking about, “What’s this
launch going to do for my next launch? How is it going to change my relationship with
the market? With my prospects? With the people who buy from me and are my new
customers? With my partners? How is it going to change my positioning? How is it
going to set up my next launch and the launch after that?”
!
As you’re looking at a launch - as you’re considering a product... a promotion... or some
angle to take - don’t just think about how much money you’re going to make. Think
about what your current clients are going to think about that. What your current
prospects are going to think about that. Think about the people that don’t buy from you
and what they’re going to think. Think about your joint venture partners and what
they’ll think. The people in your market just observing the whole market - observing
who is the expert... who is the guru... who has the positioning - what are they going to
think.
!
I’ve seen people screw this up over and over. A classic thing is someone will sell a
product, they’ll sell a bunch of that product, and then three months later they’ll give it
away for free or they’ll make it a bonus for some other offer. They’ll promote someone
else and they’ll give it away for free. They’ll do one of those dollar offers where you can
get the product for a dollar, “pay $1.00 and we’ll put you into the membership site.”
!
How do all those people that bought your product a month ago that paid a lot of money
for that product feel when you give that product away for free?
!
You have to think more long term.
!
You don’t always want to be thinking about a year from now, because you do have to
bring in the money now. You have to build the sales now. You have to take care of your
partners now. You have to take care of your clients now. But how is it going to set you
up?

PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 4 of 5 --

!
I love to get outdoors. I love to ski, I love to mountain bike, I love to white water
kayak. In almost any one of those activities, almost any kind of sport, the further you
can extend your horizon, you win. You just absolutely win and you will do better.
!
What I mean by extend your horizon is that if you’re skiing... if you’re going down the
slopes... you don’t want to be looking three feet in front of your skis. If you’re on a
mountain bike, you don’t want to be looking three feet in front of your front wheel. In a
kayak, you don’t want to be looking just in front of your bow. You want to look further
out. You want to be looking not at the next move, but two or three turns down the
path, down the river, down the slope.
That’s what the true experts do. They’re not reacting to whatever rock is in front of
them, they’re reacting to what’s coming in two or three turns down the path. That’s
how you judge an expert, that’s what the experts do. That’s how you become an
expert, by extending your horizon.
!
It’s the same way in your business. You don’t want to look at just, “What am I going to
be sending to my people tomorrow?” Yes, you have to get that done, you have to know
what you’re doing. But you want to extend your horizon. How is this email going to set
up the email next week? How’s that going to set up the video next month? How is it
going to set up the product two or three products from now?
!
You don’t want to obsess about this, but the further you extend your horizon the better
you are. The battle is won before you get on the battlefield. That was true when Sun
Tzu said it thousands of years ago, and it’s still true. Strategy is what wins in the long
run.
As we go through this module, we’re going to dig deep into the core PLF strategies and
tactics. We’re going to put the core pieces and ingredients of your launch, of your
business, in place. Those core ingredients are going to work for all of your launches
going forward. You’re going to tweak them as you go forward. But this is the hard work
that’s going to set up all of your launches.
!
In the next video, I have the number one cornerstone strategy that makes all of
Product Launch Formula work.
